skill.name: appflowy-api
skill.description: AppFlowy Cloud/GoTrue API authentication and workflow automation for self-hosted AppFlowy instances. Includes token management, workspace operations, document manipulation, database CRUD, search, and Y.js collab data processing.
skill.summary: Python toolkit for AppFlowy Cloud API integration with authentication, document management, database operations, and collaborative editing support.
skill.author: BaloneGit

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 314 static findings across 42 files. All detected patterns are false positives: external commands are legitimate subprocess calls to trusted local Node.js scripts for Y.js CRDT processing; network access is for AppFlowy API calls to user-controlled endpoints; environment file access is opt-in only via explicit --env flag; filesystem operations are for reading stdin and template files. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized access. This is a legitimate API client toolkit for self-hosted AppFlowy automation.

medium_findings.title: Subprocess Execution Without Input Sanitization
medium_findings.description: Python subprocess.run() calls execute Node.js scripts with JSON input via stdin. While input is structured JSON, there is no explicit validation of payload structure before passing to external process. Risk is mitigated by controlled script paths and local-only execution.

medium_findings.title: Opt-In Environment File Access
medium_findings.description: The skill reads .env files only when explicitly invoked with --env <path> flag. This is documented behavior but users should be aware that passing --env will load environment variables from the specified file.

low_findings.title: Bash Code Examples in Documentation
low_findings.description: Markdown documentation files contain shell command examples using backticks. These are documentation examples, not executable code. No security risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: Documentation and example config files contain hardcoded URLs including internal IP addresses (10.60.0.189). These are examples for self-hosted deployments and do not represent external network calls to untrusted endpoints.

low_findings.title: Y.js CRDT Processing via Node.js
low_findings.description: Node.js scripts use Y.js library to process collaborative document state (CRDT operations). The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are false positives from the static scanner; Y.js uses standard encoding for update vectors, not encryption.

low_findings.title: Standard Python Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Code reads configuration from os.environ for APPFLOWY_BASE_URL, API_EXTERNAL_URL, etc. This is standard Python practice for configuration management.

dangerous_patterns.title: Subprocess Execution Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: subprocess.run() executes Node.js scripts with JSON input via stdin. Scripts are local, paths are hardcoded, and input is structured JSON. Mitigated by: (1) Script paths are relative to __file__, (2) No user-controlled command arguments, (3) Input is JSON-encoded, not shell commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Requests to User-Controlled Endpoints
dangerous_patterns.description: urllib.request makes HTTP requests to user-specified base_url and gotrue_url endpoints. This is the intended functionality for an API client. Users must ensure they trust the endpoints they configure.

content.value_statement: Eliminate manual AppFlowy API calls with this Python toolkit. Handle authentication, document operations, database management, and collaborative editing in self-hosted AppFlowy environments.
actual_capabilities: Authenticate with AppFlowy Cloud using GoTrue OAuth via email/password
List and query workspaces, databases, and document views
Create and manipulate document blocks and page views
Perform CRUD operations on database grids and rows
Process Y.js collaborative document state for bulk operations
limitations: Requires self-hosted AppFlowy Cloud instance with API access
API compatibility tied to AppFlowy Cloud version 0.12.3
Y.js collab features require Node.js runtime for subprocess execution
No support for real-time WebSocket operations or live collaboration

use_cases.title: Migrate Content to AppFlowy
use_cases.description: Transfer existing documentation and structured data from legacy systems to self-hosted AppFlowy. Create workspaces, document hierarchies, and populate database grids programmatically using Python scripts.

use_cases.title: Bulk Update Document Metadata
use_cases.description: Apply systematic changes across multiple documents and databases. Update field values, add new columns, or restructure data using template-based grid operations without manual editing.

use_cases.title: Integrate AppFlowy with CI/CD Pipelines
use_cases.description: Automate document generation and updates as part of deployment workflows. Create release notes, update user documentation, or sync database records from external data sources.

prompt_templates.title: Get Authentication Token
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the appflowy-api skill to obtain an access token for my self-hosted AppFlowy instance. My email is user@example.com and password is mypass123. The GoTrue URL is http://localhost:9999.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time setup to authenticate with AppFlowy Cloud

prompt_templates.title: List All Workspaces
prompt_templates.prompt: Using appflowy-api, list all available workspaces in my AppFlowy Cloud instance. I already have an access token: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
prompt_templates.scenario: Discover workspace IDs before performing operations

prompt_templates.title: Create Document from Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use appflowy-api to create a new document in workspace abc123 using the user management template from references/templates/user_management_doc.json. My token is available in the config.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate structured documents from predefined templates

prompt_templates.title: Update Database Grid Records
prompt_templates.prompt: With appflowy-api, upsert rows into database def456 in workspace abc123. Use the fitness plan template at references/templates/fitness_plan.example.json. Update existing rows and add new ones based on the template structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Synchronize external data with AppFlowy database grids

output_examples.input: Get my AppFlowy authentication token for user@test.com with password testpass123 at GoTrue URL http://localhost:9999
List all workspaces in my AppFlowy Cloud instance using this token: eyJhbGc...
Search for 'API authentication' in workspace abc123

best_practices: Store AppFlowy URLs and credentials in config files rather than command-line arguments. Use references/config.example.json as a template and avoid hardcoding sensitive values in scripts.
Run the doctor.py script first to verify connectivity and version compatibility before performing bulk operations. This prevents partial updates due to API mismatches.
Test API operations with read-only commands like list-workspaces or search before executing write operations that modify documents or databases.
anti_patterns: Do not pass passwords directly as command-line arguments in production environments. Use --env flag with a .env file or set environment variables for credentials.
Avoid performing database grid updates without backing up existing data. The upsert operations modify records in-place and mistakes can overwrite valuable information.
Never share access tokens or include them in Git repositories. Tokens expire but can be abused before expiration if leaked.

faq.question: Does this skill work with AppFlowy Cloud hosted services?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for self-hosted AppFlowy Cloud instances. It may work with hosted AppFlowy if you have API access, but it is primarily tested against self-hosted deployments.

faq.question: Why does the skill require Node.js for some operations?
faq.answer: Collaborative editing features use Y.js (a CRDT library) to process document state. The Node.js scripts handle complex Y.js operations that are difficult to implement in Python alone.

faq.question: Is my .env file automatically loaded?
faq.answer: No. Environment file loading is opt-in only. You must explicitly pass --env <path> to read variables from a .env file. By default, the skill uses command-line arguments or environment variables.

faq.question: What AppFlowy Cloud version is supported?
faq.answer: The skill is tested against AppFlowy Cloud version 0.12.3. The doctor.py script checks the /api/health endpoint and warns if versions do not match.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes a unified entry point (appflowy_skill.py) that makes it easy to invoke from Claude Code. Run commands like 'python scripts/appflowy_skill.py list' to see available operations.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot connection errors?
faq.answer: First, run doctor.py with your credentials to diagnose issues. Common problems include firewall rules blocking ports 80/443, incorrect base URLs, or AppFlowy Cloud service not running. Check container connectivity if using Docker.
